turns.text: Summarize key takeaways these scratch notes into coherent, well-written bulleted sentences.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
- These notes appear to be about a conference on innovation and entrepreneurship.
- Speakers discussed the importance of being adaptable and embracing change in order to succeed in business.
- The idea of "failing forward" was also emphasized, encouraging entrepreneurs to learn from their failures and use them to improve.
- Networking was identified as a crucial part of building and growing a successful business.
- Speakers also highlighted the importance of being aware of industry trends and staying up-to-date on new technologies and developments.
- The need for diversity and inclusivity was also emphasized, both in terms of hiring practices and customer outreach.
- Overall, the conference appeared to provide valuable insights and advice for aspiring and established entrepreneurs.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Summarize: Project Dispatch Management Presentation (Palladium)
5/8/2023

HGGC Attendees: 
•	Alex Funk, Managing Director (Palladium): <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS> - AF
•	Walter Love, Senior Associate (Palladium): <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS> - WL
•	Mark Horvick, Managing Director (Northborne): <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS> - MH
•	Mat Marcussen, VP (Northborne): <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS> - MM
AGI Attendees: 
•	Matt Greenstein - MG
•	Tray Chamberlin - TC
•	Luke Mraz - LM
•	Calvin Truong - CT


OPENING / INTRODUCTIONS	
•	Palladium Leader (not sure of name?)
o	4 verticals: Healthcare (HC), Services, Industrials, Consumer
o	Been here 8 years
o	Prior: Technology and service offerings; HC banking as well
o	15 years of experience
•	Palladium Leader (Jeff Wiggin?)
o	Previously: HC
o	Family business background
o	Always enjoyed working with family founders – not all PE firms handle approprioately
•	Palladium Leader (Alissa Lafluer?)
o	Associate with Palladium
o	Chicago undergrad – from Lake Zurich, IL
•	Palladium Leader (‘holding company guy’ -- not sure of name?)
o	Managing Director of Wholesale Capital
o	10 years at Palladium
o	Focused on ‘HC Services’
•	Palladium Leader (not sure of name?)
o	NJ guy
o	Helps with Palladium investments; Advisor to the company
o	Uses connections and network to help investments become successful


PALLADIUM HISTORY
•	Year founded: 1997
•	$3B AUM
•	Typically $50M-$150M investment size
•	70% of our deals are family-owned businesses
•	38 platforms investments; 160 add-ons (4-5 on average)
•	Oriented around Growth Equity investor
•	Goal: Building business without thinking about cap structure
•	HC combined with Tech – number of deals in that area
•	Analog deals: Everdriven (CEO Mitch)
o	Parallels to Dispatch: important mission, special needs kids, transportation
o	Business is recurring; nice margins; solving really important pain point
o	Uniquely interesting profile – organic growth
o	Palladium invested in Sales Team, Tech Team – thought about next-gen service offering
	3-4 years in, significant growth
	Investors are very helpful
o	CEO Mitch
o	Based out of Denver, CO
o	(Northborne Leader lives close to CEO Mitch)
•	We believe we can inject capital and resources to help accelerate growth.
•	Everdriven was connected with HealthConnect (partnership) and tech-enabled services (software) to get going.
•	Jeff worked on Everdriven deal – Jeff would work on NORA

(39 participants on the call)
(“You need an army to do this kind of stuff”)

ADVSIRO GROUPS
•	Bain
•	Marwood – HC regulatory consulting (Mark Slomiany)
•	West Monroe – Tech/IT consulting
•	Lending groups
o	Household names, LBOs, etc.
•	ESG Solutions Team (Carlos, Deborah)
•	Today’s call is to talk about how we can sustain growth potential
o	Case: always 2-3 weeks in, and missing fuel

CEO DON JONES
•	Day 1
•	Focus: client relationships; aviation (owns 2 certificates); in aviation since college (since 1983)
•	Minority owner of Air Response
•	2006 sold to competitor > sold to GMR > exists under Global Medical Response
o	Rolled into Grandview deal
o	ASI rolls into another deal
•	Left in 2009
•	Also deals with: insurance; vehicle purchasing & contracting; “button pusher”

COLE BACHELDER
•	Co-founder and President
•	Started Transport – logistics and medical transport + medical assistance co
o	30MM company; Cole exited in 2016; sell to 2x Key Managers
o	NORA leans on Transport heavily
o	Cole is no longer a shareholder in that old business
•	Cole’s focus: back of the house; manage Dispatch; manage IT & Infrastructure; oversees administration; Accounts Receivables – all of the stuff in Illinois
•	Cole is inside guy; Don is outside guy
SCOTT DEMCZYSZYN
•	Ukrainian last name
•	Background: HC; not an owner like Don and Cole
•	Worked as hospital paramedic > promoted to Director of Department > moved to Pennsylvania opportunity, hired to run Pennsylvania business
•	1996 – started as transplant coordinator > performed everything end-to-end (21 years) > became Director of Coordination department > served on lots of industry committees, OPO committees, national committees
o	OPO community is very tight-knit; lots of long-standing relationships
•	For ‘Gift of Life’, he managed transportation
o	Initially, ‘Gift of Life’ said we don’t need you > driver had accident > then NORA won contract
o	Process was internally: if you’re in NJ, call these local providers. If you’re in TX, you call these providers.
	Coordinators should focus on coordinating and not on logistics/transportation
•	Organ transplantation is a mission-driven process
o	“Word is your bond” type of business
•	Don recruited Scott into business

ORG CHART
•	715 employees – highly defensible operation
•	Company mantra: NORA is the steward of the organ/tissue gift
o	Palladium: “this is your Day 1 to be part of this special process”
o	(only had 2 tchotchkes to pass out)
•	Q: difference between FT and PT employees?
o	A: 53 people in the office – FT employees
o	A: Drivers are employees on a per diem basis
o	A: 43 Driver Supervisors
o	A: Every employee sends shift to Supervisor > Supervisor approves
o	A: some drivers drive everyday
•	Ideal Talent profile: ex-Paramedic; ex-service men; people who are driven by mission
•	Couriers: focus on blood samples
o	Couriers that are based at hospital (to/from hospitals)
•	Q: how have you kept Team Culture as you’ve scaled?
o	A: recruiting men/women who join this team as second career (not second job)
o	A: law enforcement, fire fighters – retire in 50s and have lots of year left in them
o	A: some of our competitors hire for a JOB and not a CAREER – “can’t believe you hand these people an ORGAN”
o	A: competitors run into huge risks with poor controls (e.g., safety checks, driving tests, training)
•	Birminham, Mobile, and _____ Alabama – just launched in 3 cities in AL
•	Don takes care of IT
•	Just hired Director of Client Relationships (gentlemen with 12 years in industry; via ‘Buckeye Transplant’; lives in Omaha; attended training in KY and AL) – saw the need for another right-hand behind Don and Cole
o	Hiring 2 more Directors of Client Relationships in the future
•	Director of Flight Operations (manages all flight) – role overseen by Don

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The Palladium project dispatch management presentation had seven attendees, and it discussed the company's history, investment goals, and focus areas. Palladium invests for growth equity, and their aim is to build businesses without thinking about cap structure. Their goal is to sustain growth potential in all their investments. NORA, their project, addresses logistics and medical transport and focuses on organ transplantation. The organizational structure includes 715 highly defensible employees who focus on recruiting ex-Paramedics, ex-service men, and people who are driven by mission. Palladium believes that they can inject capital and resources to accelerate growth.
